title: The Number Type
description: Handle integers, decimals, and math operations.

slides.title: All Numbers Are One 💯
slides.content: Unlike Java or C#, which have separate types for integers (`int`) and decimals (`float`), JavaScript and TypeScript have just one single type for all numeric values: `number`.

slides.title: Defining Numbers
slides.content: Every number annotation is exactly the same, no matter the value:

quizData.question: If you want to store the value `3.1415`, what type should you use?
quizData.explanation: TypeScript uses the single `number` type for both whole numbers and decimals.

slides.title: Hex, Binary, and Octal 💻
slides.content:
The `number` type also officially supports hexadecimal, binary, and octal literals!

Just start your number with `0x`, `0b`, or `0o`, respectively.

slides.title: Number Math
slides.content: When doing mathematical operations, TypeScript ensures you only add, subtract, multiply, or divide other numbers.
